name: United States
description: The USA — states, capitals, and Americana

questions.q: Which is the largest U.S. state by area?

source.notes: About 1.7 million square kilometers — more than twice the size of Texas.

questions.q: What is the national bird of the United States?

source.notes: Adopted as the national emblem in 1782.

questions.q: Which was the 50th and most recent state admitted to the Union?

source.notes: Admitted August 21, 1959, about seven months after Alaska.

questions.q: How many boroughs make up New York City?

source.notes: Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, The Bronx, Staten Island.

questions.q: Which state was first to ratify the U.S. Constitution, in 1787?

source.notes: Nicknamed 'The First State' for this reason.

questions.q: What is the capital city of the United States?

source.notes: Washington, D.C. has been the capital since 1800.

questions.q: Hollywood, the center of the U.S. film industry, is in which state?

source.notes: Neighborhood of Los Angeles; film industry moved there around 1910.

questions.q: Which state is nicknamed 'The Lone Star State'?

source.notes: References the single star on the Republic of Texas flag.

questions.q: According to legend, Peter Minuit bought Manhattan Island from Native Americans in 1626 for goods estimated at what value?

source.notes: Actually paid in trade goods worth about 60 guilders — famously pegged at $24 by 19th-century historians.

questions.q: The iconic geyser Old Faithful is located in which U.S. national park?

source.notes: Erupts roughly every 90 minutes.

questions.q: In 1955, Rosa Parks sparked the civil rights movement by refusing to give up her bus seat in which city?

source.notes: December 1, 1955; triggered the Montgomery bus boycott.

questions.q: Which California city is nicknamed 'The City by the Bay'?

source.notes: Named for its position on San Francisco Bay.

questions.q: Which New England city is known by the nickname 'Beantown'?

source.notes: Reference to traditional Boston baked beans.

questions.q: What is the capital of California?

source.notes: Sacramento has been California's capital since 1854.

questions.q: What is the capital of the state of New York?

source.notes: Capital since 1797.

questions.q: Which U.S. state lies just 90 miles north of Cuba at its closest point?

source.notes: Key West, Florida is approximately 90 miles from Cuba.

questions.q: Which state followed Delaware as the second to ratify the U.S. Constitution?

source.notes: Ratified December 12, 1787.

questions.q: Which U.S. state has the longest total coastline?

source.notes: Alaska has more coastline than all other U.S. states combined.

questions.q: Santa Fe, the oldest continuously inhabited U.S. state capital, has served in that role since what year?

source.notes: Founded as a Spanish colonial capital in 1610.

questions.q: What is the capital of Vermont?

source.notes: Smallest U.S. state capital by population (about 8,000).

questions.q: What is the capital of Nevada?

source.notes: Named after Kit Carson; one of the smaller capitals by population.

questions.q: Construction of the White House began in which year?

source.notes: Cornerstone laid October 13, 1792; first occupied by John Adams in 1800.
